label: true-dup
a: All timestamps stored in the database are UTC. No exceptions. Timezone conversion from UTC to user local time happens only in the frontend at display time.
b: Established the UTC-only storage rule: the backend stores all datetimes in UTC. The API always returns UTC. The frontend is responsible for converting to the user local timezone for display. Mixing timezones in storage is a source of subtle bugs, especially around DST transitions.
reviewer_notes: Both state the same UTC-only storage rule with frontend-side display conversion.
